name: Strawberry Cheesecake 1
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush fresh strawberries or thaw frozen unsweetened ones. Mix with 1/3 cup evaporated milk (use a scant 2 tablespoons powdered skim milk and fill to the 1/3 mark).
2. Preheat oven to 400°F. Melt margarine in a 9-inch pan. Add crumbs and mix. Press mixture over the bottom of the pan.
3. Bake for 5 to 7 minutes, then cool.
4. Sift cottage cheese or puree in a blender until smooth. Add milk and stir until smooth. Chill.
5. In a saucepan, mix 1 1/2 teaspoons gelatin and 1 tablespoon sugar with juice and zest. Heat over low heat, stirring constantly until gelatin is dissolved. Remove from heat and let stand at room temperature.
6. In a medium bowl, beat egg whites with salt until stiff. Fold in gelatin and cottage cheese mixture. Pour over crumbs.
7. Refrigerate until set before adding the glaze.
8. Mix remaining gelatin (1/2 tablespoon) with water and 1 tablespoon sugar. Heat until gelatin is dissolved. Stir in strawberries. Pour mixture over cheesecake and spread with a spatula.
9. Refrigerate until firm.
Nutritional Information:
1/8 recipe, 120 calories, 1/2 starch, 1 lean meat, 1/2 fruit exchange
7.8 gm protein, 4.1 gm fat, 13.4 gm carbohydrate, 253.6 mg sodium,
207.2 mg potassium, 1.2 gm fiber, 3 mg cholesterol.
Source: American Diabetes Association, Family Cookbook Vol 1, 1987
Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Nov 93
